name: flow-replay-under-changed-policy
description: Tier 2: run 1 records a step under a policy with no retry table (attempt budget 1). Run 2 reconfigures the engine with a new retry policy (attempts=3) before resuming: the recorded step still substitutes verbatim with its ORIGINAL attempts=1, completely unaffected by the new policy, while a brand-new live step in the same run is retried per the NEW policy (attempts=3) before it is exhausted. Recorded steps and live policy never contaminate each other (spec 4.3 tier boundary). Stubs skip tier-2 scenarios; the real core runs this in crates/keel-core/tests/flows_conformance.rs.
